sources.title: Caveman
sources.description: Public skills repository with compact communication and workflow skills.

sources.title: Matt Pocock Skills
sources.description: Composable public engineering skills with setup and workflow helpers.

sources.title: Context7
sources.description: Public skill and MCP source for up-to-date documentation retrieval.

installables.name: ctx7
installables.title: Context7 CLI
installables.description: Context7 CLI for up-to-date documentation retrieval via ctx7 commands.
runtimeChecks: ctx7 is callable from the host runtime.
CONTEXT7_API_KEY is optional and only needed for authenticated access.
setupHints: Store CONTEXT7_API_KEY outside the repository if you need authenticated access.
Use ctx7 library <name> to search for libraries.
Use ctx7 docs <libraryId> <query> to retrieve documentation.

installables.name: context7
installables.title: Context7 MCP
installables.description: Context7 MCP server for fresh documentation retrieval.
runtimeChecks: MCP command can be launched from the host runtime.
CONTEXT7_API_KEY is optional and only needed for authenticated access.
setupHints: Store CONTEXT7_API_KEY outside the repository if you need authenticated access.

sources.title: GhidraMCP
sources.description: Bridge-based MCP for Ghidra reverse-engineering sessions.
setupHints: Install the latest GhidraMCP extension zip inside Ghidra via File > Install Extensions.
Enable GhidraMCPPlugin in Ghidra and confirm the HTTP server port under Tool Options.
Keep a local checkout or cached copy of bridge_mcp_ghidra.py available to the host runtime.
Python 3 and the MCP Python SDK must be available on the host running the bridge script.

installables.name: ghidra
installables.title: GhidraMCP
installables.description: Bridge script for connecting MCP clients to a running Ghidra HTTP server.
runtimeChecks: python is callable from the host runtime.
Ghidra HTTP server responds at the configured URL.
setupHints: Point the MCP bridge at the running Ghidra HTTP server URL, typically http://127.0.0.1:8080/.
The bridge can run in stdio mode by default or SSE mode when explicitly requested.

sources.title: Spec Kit
sources.description: Official GitHub Spec Kit CLI for upstream spec-driven workflows.
setupHints: Spec Kit is optional upstream tooling and does not replace this repo's internal spec-driven profile.
Use uv for the recommended install path, or pipx when uv is unavailable.
Bootstrap mutates the selected repo and should only run after explicit operator confirmation.

installables.name: specify
installables.title: Spec Kit
installables.description: Official Specify CLI installed from github/spec-kit.
runtimeChecks: uv or pipx is available for installation.
specify version succeeds from the host runtime.
setupHints: Do not install unrelated PyPI packages named specify-cli; use the GitHub repo source only.
Windows bootstrap defaults to PowerShell scripts.
Use specify init --here only inside the explicitly selected repo.

sources.title: AFT
sources.description: Agent Framework Tools — tool replacements and aft_ tools for OpenCode.
setupHints: Use npx @cortexkit/aft@latest setup for full AFT setup.
This installable only activates the OpenCode plugin adapter (@cortexkit/aft-opencode).

installables.name: aft
installables.title: AFT OpenCode Plugin
installables.description: AFT agent framework tools for OpenCode. Adds tool replacements and aft_ tools.
setupHints: AFT adds tool replacements and custom aft_ tools to OpenCode.
Coexists with RTK and either context manager.

sources.title: Magic Context
sources.description: OpenCode context management plugin — disables built-in compaction for manual context control.
setupHints: Magic Context conflicts with DCP. Only one context manager can be active at a time.
Activating Magic Context disables compaction.auto and compaction.prune in opencode.jsonc.

installables.name: magic-context
installables.title: Magic Context Plugin
installables.description: OpenCode context manager — disables auto compaction and pruning for manual context control.
setupHints: Disables compaction.auto and compaction.prune when activated.
Conflicts with DCP. If DCP is active, it will be deactivated automatically.

sources.title: RTK
sources.description: RTK CLI tool and OpenCode plugin integration.
setupHints: RTK requires the rtk CLI to be installed first.
Activation runs rtk init -g --opencode --auto-patch.
Deactivation runs rtk init -g --uninstall only when Elegy performed the activation.

installables.name: rtk
installables.title: RTK Plugin
installables.description: RTK CLI tool and OpenCode integration. Activates via rtk init and verifies with rtk --version.
setupHints: Install rtk CLI first, then activate this installable.
Deactivation uses rtk init -g --uninstall.

sources.title: DCP
sources.description: Dynamic context pruning plugin for OpenCode. Conflicts with Magic Context.
setupHints: DCP conflicts with Magic Context. Only one context manager can be active at a time.
Install globally with: opencode plugin @tarquinen/opencode-dcp@latest --global.

installables.name: dcp
installables.title: DCP Plugin
installables.description: Dynamic context pruning for OpenCode. Mutually exclusive with Magic Context.
setupHints: Conflicts with Magic Context. If Magic Context is active, it will be deactivated automatically.
The DCP plugin is @tarquinen/opencode-dcp.
